title: I've Never Been So Happy to Be Back in INDIA

This majestic monument is situated in an enviable location on the shore washed constantly by the waves. From Arjuna's penance, a straight path towards the sea leads one to the structural temple. Welcome to this historical retreat and an ancient seaport of the Pallavas, who ruled from nearby Kanchipuram between the 3rd and 8th centuries AD. Situated 60 km south of Chennai, the monuments of Mahabalipuram, hewn out of rocks, attracts thousands of connoisseurs of art from all over the world. Though ravaged by sea, wind, and time, these sculptural treasures speak volumes about the magnificent heritage of ancient Dravidian art and temple architecture. Ishwara Temple at Mamallapuram, a small west-facing structural temple built on the top of a hillock.
